# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI Release Workflow
on: 
    push:
        tags: ['v*'] # Using 'git push' tag in versioning system (i.e., v0.1, v2.3) is a good practice to trigger the workflow automatically on any tagged commit  
jobs:
     build-and-release: 
          runs-on: ubuntu-latest   
         # you may want separate jobs for building and testing if your app needs them (not shown here).       
           steps:     
            - name: Checkout repository      
              uses: actions/checkout@v2  
            
            - run: npm install  && \npm login --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org    # Authenticate NPM for the project using an access token (NPM_TOKEN is a GitHub Secret in this case)    
           with:      
                npm-token: ${{ secrets.NPM_AUTH_TOKEN }}  // This line should be removed after CI/CD setup   # Store NPX auth Token as secret to avoid exposing it     
             - name: Node Version   
               uses: actions/setup-node@v1      
              with:    
                node-version: '12'  // or any other version if needed.       
            - run : npm publish --access public   # Publish package to NPMJS
